Implements a workout summary window for Pebble firmware, handling UI elements and activity type updates.

# Purpose
The code defines a `WorkoutSummaryWindow` structure and its associated functions to manage a user interface window for displaying workout summaries. This window is part of a larger application, likely related to fitness or health tracking, and it provides functionality to display different types of workout activities, such as open workouts, walking, and running. The window includes UI components like an action bar, status bar, and base layer, and it uses icons and text to represent the current workout activity.

The code includes functions to create and manage the `WorkoutSummaryWindow`, handle user interactions through button clicks, and update the display based on the selected workout activity. The [`workout_summary_window_create`](<#workout_summary_window_create>) function initializes the window and its components, while the [`prv_up_click_handler`](<#prv_up_click_handler>) and [`prv_down_click_handler`](<#prv_down_click_handler>) functions manage user input to start a workout countdown or select a different workout, respectively. The [`prv_render_activity_type`](<#prv_render_activity_type>) function is responsible for drawing the activity type icon and name on the window. The code also includes memory management functions to ensure resources are properly allocated and freed.

# Data Structures

---
### WorkoutSummaryWindow
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - ``window``: A `Window` object that represents the main window of the workout summary.
    - ``action_bar``: An `ActionBarLayer` object that provides a UI action bar for user interactions.
    - ``status_layer``: A `StatusBarLayer` object that displays status information at the top of the window.
    - ``base_layer``: A `Layer` object that serves as the base layer for rendering content.
    - ``action_bar_start``: A pointer to a `GBitmap` object for the start icon in the action bar.
    - ``action_bar_more``: A pointer to a `GBitmap` object for the more icon in the action bar.
    - ``activity_type``: An `ActivitySessionType` enum that indicates the type of activity session.
    - ``icon``: A pointer to a `KinoReel` object that represents the icon for the activity type.
    - ``name``: A constant character pointer that holds the name of the activity.
    - ``start_workout_cb``: A `StartWorkoutCallback` function pointer for starting a workout.
    - ``select_workout_cb``: A `SelectWorkoutCallback` function pointer for selecting a workout.
- **Description**: Represents a window structure for displaying a workout summary, including UI components such as an action bar, status bar, and base layer. It manages icons and names for different activity types and provides callback functions for starting and selecting workouts.


# Functions

---
### prv\_get\_icon\_for\_activity<!-- {{#callable:prv_get_icon_for_activity}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/workout/workout_summary.c#L61>)

Selects and returns a `KinoReel` icon based on the given `ActivitySessionType`.
- **Inputs**:
    - `type`: An `ActivitySessionType` enum value that specifies the type of activity session.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Use a `switch` statement to evaluate the `type` parameter.
    - If `type` is `ActivitySessionType_Open`, call `kino_reel_create_with_resource` with `RESOURCE_ID_WORKOUT_APP_WORKOUT` and return the result.
    - If `type` is `ActivitySessionType_Walk`, call `kino_reel_create_with_resource` with `RESOURCE_ID_WORKOUT_APP_WALK` and return the result.
    - For `ActivitySessionType_Run` and any other default case, call `kino_reel_create_with_resource` with `RESOURCE_ID_WORKOUT_APP_RUN` and return the result.
- **Output**: A pointer to a `KinoReel` object that represents the icon for the specified activity type.


---
### prv\_render\_activity\_type<!-- {{#callable:prv_render_activity_type}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/workout/workout_summary.c#L76>)

Renders an activity type icon and name on a specified layer using a given graphics context.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``ctx``: A pointer to the `GContext` used for drawing operations.
    - ``layer``: A pointer to the `Layer` on which the activity type will be rendered.
    - ``icon``: A pointer to a `KinoReel` object representing the icon to be drawn.
    - ``name``: A constant character pointer to the name of the activity type to be displayed.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Retrieve the system font `FONT_KEY_GOTHIC_24_BOLD` and set text overflow mode to `GTextOverflowModeWordWrap` and alignment to `GTextAlignmentCenter`.
    - Calculate the right-left margin using `PBL_IF_RECT_ELSE` macro to determine the margin based on the device shape.
    - Inset the `layer` bounds by the calculated margin to get the `drawing_rect`.
    - Get the size of the `icon` using `kino_reel_get_size` and calculate the `icon_x` and `icon_y` positions for the icon within the `drawing_rect`.
    - Draw the `icon` at the calculated position using `kino_reel_draw`.
    - Calculate the `name_x` and `name_y` positions for the name text within the `drawing_rect`.
    - Create a [`GRect`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GRect>) for the name text and set the text color using `graphics_context_set_text_color`.
    - Draw the `name` text within the `name_rect` using `graphics_draw_text` with the specified font, overflow mode, and alignment.
- **Output**: No return value; the function performs drawing operations on the provided `GContext`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`grect_inset`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.c.md#grect_inset>)
    - [`GEdgeInsets`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GEdgeInsets>)
    - [`GPoint`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GPoint>)
    - [`GRect`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GRect>)

### prv\_base\_layer\_update\_proc<!-- {{#callable:prv_base_layer_update_proc}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/workout/workout_summary.c#L99>)

Updates the base layer of a workout summary window by rendering the activity type icon and name.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``layer``: A pointer to the `Layer` structure that needs updating.
    - ``ctx``: A pointer to the `GContext` used for drawing operations.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Retrieve the `WorkoutSummaryWindow` associated with the given `layer` by accessing the user data of the window containing the `layer`.
    - Call [`prv_render_activity_type`](<#prv_render_activity_type>) to draw the activity type icon and name on the `layer` using the `ctx`, `summary_window->icon`, and `summary_window->name`.
- **Output**: No return value; the function updates the graphical content of the specified `layer`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`window_get_user_data`](<../../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_get_user_data>)
    - [`layer_get_window`](<../../../applib/ui/layer.c.md#layer_get_window>)
    - [`prv_render_activity_type`](<#prv_render_activity_type>)

### prv\_up\_click\_handler<!-- {{#callable:prv_up_click_handler}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/workout/workout_summary.c#L108>)

Handles the 'up' button click event to start a workout countdown and remove the current window from the stack.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``recognizer``: A reference to the click recognizer that detected the button press.
    - ``context``: A pointer to the `WorkoutSummaryWindow` structure, which contains the context for the workout summary window.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Cast the `context` to a `WorkoutSummaryWindow` pointer and store it in `summary_window`.
    - Call [`workout_countdown_start`](<workout_countdown.c.md#workout_countdown_start>) with `summary_window->activity_type` and `summary_window->start_workout_cb` to initiate the workout countdown.
    - Remove `summary_window->window` from the window stack without animating the removal.
- **Output**: No return value; the function performs actions based on the input context.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`workout_countdown_start`](<workout_countdown.c.md#workout_countdown_start>)


---
### prv\_down\_click\_handler<!-- {{#callable:prv_down_click_handler}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/workout/workout_summary.c#L116>)

Handles the down button click event by pushing the workout selection screen.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``recognizer``: A reference to the click recognizer that identifies the button click event.
    - ``context``: A pointer to the `WorkoutSummaryWindow` structure, which contains the callback for selecting a workout.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Cast the `context` pointer to a `WorkoutSummaryWindow` pointer named `summary_window`.
    - Call the [`workout_selection_push`](<workout_selection.c.md#workout_selection_push>) function with `summary_window->select_workout_cb` as the argument to push the workout selection screen.
- **Output**: No return value.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`workout_selection_push`](<workout_selection.c.md#workout_selection_push>)

### prv\_click\_config\_provider<!-- {{#callable:prv_click_config_provider}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/workout/workout_summary.c#L122>)

Configures click event handlers for the UP and DOWN buttons in a window.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``context``: A pointer to user-defined data that is passed to the click handlers.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Sets the click context for the UP button using `window_set_click_context` with `BUTTON_ID_UP` and `context`.
    - Sets the click context for the DOWN button using `window_set_click_context` with `BUTTON_ID_DOWN` and `context`.
    - Subscribes the UP button to a single click event with `window_single_click_subscribe` and assigns `prv_up_click_handler` as the handler.
    - Subscribes the DOWN button to a single click event with `window_single_click_subscribe` and assigns `prv_down_click_handler` as the handler.
- **Output**: No return value; the function configures button click handlers.

### prv\_window\_unload\_handler<!-- {{#callable:prv_window_unload_handler}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/workout/workout_summary.c#L129>)

Releases resources and deinitializes components associated with a `WorkoutSummaryWindow` when a window is unloaded.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``window``: A pointer to the `Window` object that is being unloaded.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Retrieve the `WorkoutSummaryWindow` user data from the `window` using [`window_get_user_data`](<../../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_get_user_data>).
    - Check if `summary_window` is not NULL to ensure it is valid.
    - If valid, destroy the `icon` using `kino_reel_destroy`.
    - Destroy the `action_bar_more` and `action_bar_start` bitmaps using `gbitmap_destroy`.
    - Deinitialize the `action_bar` using [`action_bar_layer_deinit`](<../../../applib/ui/action_bar_layer.c.md#action_bar_layer_deinit>).
    - Deinitialize the `status_layer` using `status_bar_layer_deinit`.
    - Deinitialize the `base_layer` using [`layer_deinit`](<../../../applib/ui/layer.c.md#layer_deinit>).
    - Deinitialize the `window` using [`window_deinit`](<../../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_deinit>).
    - Free internationalization resources using `i18n_free_all`.
    - Free the `summary_window` memory using `app_free`.
- **Output**: No return value; the function performs cleanup operations.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`window_get_user_data`](<../../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_get_user_data>)
    - [`action_bar_layer_deinit`](<../../../applib/ui/action_bar_layer.c.md#action_bar_layer_deinit>)
    - [`layer_deinit`](<../../../applib/ui/layer.c.md#layer_deinit>)
    - [`window_deinit`](<../../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_deinit>)

### workout\_summary\_window\_create<!-- {{#callable:workout_summary_window_create}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/workout/workout_summary.c#L147>)

Creates and initializes a `WorkoutSummaryWindow` with specified activity type and callback functions.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``activity_type``: Specifies the type of activity session for the workout summary.
    - ``start_workout_cb``: Callback function to start a workout.
    - ``select_workout_cb``: Callback function to select a workout.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Allocate memory for a `WorkoutSummaryWindow` structure.
    - Assign the provided callback functions to the `summary_window`.
    - Initialize the `window` component of `summary_window` with a name and set its user data.
    - Set the background color and window handlers for the `window`.
    - Adjust the bounds of the `base_layer` and initialize it, setting an update procedure and adding it to the window's layer.
    - Initialize the `status_layer`, set its colors, and add it to the window's layer.
    - For rectangular displays, adjust the frame of the `status_layer`.
    - Initialize the `action_bar`, set its context and click configuration provider, and add it to the window.
    - Create and set icons for the `action_bar` buttons using resources.
    - Update the activity type of the `summary_window` using the provided `activity_type`.
- **Output**: Returns a pointer to the newly created `WorkoutSummaryWindow`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`window_init`](<../../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_init>)
    - [`window_set_user_data`](<../../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_user_data>)
    - [`window_set_background_color`](<../../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_background_color>)
    - [`window_set_window_handlers`](<../../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_window_handlers>)
    - [`status_bar_layer_get_layer`](<../../../applib/ui/status_bar_layer.c.md#status_bar_layer_get_layer>)
    - [`layer_set_frame`](<../../../applib/ui/layer.c.md#layer_set_frame>)
    - [`action_bar_layer_init`](<../../../applib/ui/action_bar_layer.c.md#action_bar_layer_init>)
    - [`action_bar_layer_set_context`](<../../../applib/ui/action_bar_layer.c.md#action_bar_layer_set_context>)
    - [`action_bar_layer_set_click_config_provider`](<../../../applib/ui/action_bar_layer.c.md#action_bar_layer_set_click_config_provider>)
    - [`action_bar_layer_add_to_window`](<../../../applib/ui/action_bar_layer.c.md#action_bar_layer_add_to_window>)
    - [`gbitmap_create_with_resource`](<../../../applib/graphics/gbitmap.c.md#gbitmap_create_with_resource>)
    - [`action_bar_layer_set_icon`](<../../../applib/ui/action_bar_layer.c.md#action_bar_layer_set_icon>)
    - [`workout_summary_update_activity_type`](<#workout_summary_update_activity_type>)

### workout\_summary\_window\_push<!-- {{#callable:workout_summary_window_push}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/workout/workout_summary.c#L200>)

Pushes the `WorkoutSummaryWindow` onto the application window stack.
- **Inputs**:
    - `summary_window`: A pointer to a `WorkoutSummaryWindow` structure that contains the window to push onto the stack.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls `app_window_stack_push` with the address of the `window` member of `summary_window` and `true` to animate the push.
- **Output**: No return value.


---
### workout\_summary\_update\_activity\_type<!-- {{#callable:workout_summary_update_activity_type}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/workout/workout_summary.c#L204>)

Updates the activity type, icon, and name in a `WorkoutSummaryWindow`.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``summary_window``: A pointer to a `WorkoutSummaryWindow` structure that holds the workout summary details.
    - ``activity_type``: An `ActivitySessionType` value that specifies the type of activity to update in the workout summary.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Assigns the `activity_type` to the `activity_type` field of `summary_window`.
    - Calls [`prv_get_icon_for_activity`](<#prv_get_icon_for_activity>) with `activity_type` to get the corresponding icon and assigns it to the `icon` field of `summary_window`.
    - Calls `workout_utils_get_name_for_activity` with `activity_type` to get the corresponding name and assigns it to the `name` field of `summary_window`.
- **Output**: No return value; the function updates the fields of the `WorkoutSummaryWindow` structure in place.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_get_icon_for_activity`](<#prv_get_icon_for_activity>)
